title: NIS2 Proportional Security Measure Verification by Tier

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 Art.21(2) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.32: NIS2 Proportional Security Measure Verification by Tier.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.32' running on nis2_control_matrix.csv (control_id, required_for_tier), ES correlation search inventory, nis2_entity_classification.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| inputlookup nis2_control_matrix.csv
| join entity_tier [
    | inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv
    | stats values(entity_tier) as entity_tiers by legal_entity_id
    | mvexpand entity_tiers
    | rename entity_tiers as entity_tier
]
| where required_for_tier="true"
| join control_id [
    | rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=0 splunk_server=local
    | search title="nis2_*"
    | eval control_id=replace(title,"nis2_","")
    | stats latest(disabled) as search_disabled by control_id
]
| where search_disabled=1 OR isnull(search_disabled)
| table control_id, entity_tier, search_disabled
description: Important entities are not required to implement every control at essential-entity intensity. This use case compares deployed detective and preventive controls against a tiered control matrix — surfacing overkill gaps (waste) and under-provision gaps (risk) relative to classification.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Encode which ES searches map to which control IDs; (2) mark controls required per tier; (3) alert when required searches are disabled; (4) review quarterly with risk committee; (5) attach matrix version to audit evidence. Lookups: `nis2_control_matrix`, `nis2_control_matrix.csv`, `nis2_entity_classification`, `nis2_entity_classification.csv`.
visualization: Matrix heatmap (tier vs control), Table (disabled required controls), Single value (coverage %). Key fields: `control_id`, `entity_tier`, `entity_tiers`, `search_disabled`.
knownFalsePositives: Operational noise during large maintenance windows, controlled exercises, or scope changes to critical entity lists.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `nis2_control_matrix.csv` (control_id, required_for_tier), ES correlation search inventory, `nis2_entity_classification.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Encode which ES searches map to which control IDs; (2) mark controls required per tier; (3) alert when required searches are disabled; (4) review quarterly with risk committee; (5) attach matrix version to audit evidence.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_control_matrix.csv
| join entity_tier [
    | inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv
    | stats values(entity_tier) as entity_tiers by legal_entity_id
    | mvexpand entity_tiers
    | rename entity_tiers as entity_tier
]
| where required_for_tier="true"
| join control_id [
    | rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=0 splunk_server=local
    | search title="nis2_*"
    | eval control_id=replace(title,"nis2_","")
    | stats latest(disabled) as search_disabled by control_id
]
| where search_disabled=1 OR isnull(search_disabled)
| table control_id, entity_tier, search_disabled
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Proportional Security Measure Verification by Tier (Art. 21(2))** — Important entities are not required to implement every control at essential-entity intensity. This use case compares deployed detective and preventive controls against a tiered control matrix — surfacing overkill gaps (waste) and under-provision gaps (risk) relative to classification.

Documented **Data sources**: `nis2_control_matrix.csv` (control_id, required_for_tier), ES correlation search inventory, `nis2_entity_classification.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where required_for_tier="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where search_disabled=1 OR isnull(search_disabled)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: control_id, entity_tier, entity_tiers, search_disabled.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Matrix heatmap (tier vs control), Table (disabled required controls), Single value (coverage %).
grandmaExplanation: We important entities are not required to implement every control at essential-entity intensity.
